title: Use Locators
description: Master different locator strategies in Playwright for robust element selection
instructions:
Write a Playwright test that demonstrates:
1. Finding elements by CSS selector
2. Finding elements by text
3. Finding elements by test ID
4. Chaining locators for nested elements
5. Filtering locators by text or state

    page.goto('https://demo.playwright.dev/todomvc')
    
    # TODO: Find input by CSS selector
    
    # TODO: Find element by placeholder text

    # TODO: Use chained locators to find nested element
    
    # TODO: Filter locators to find specific item
    pass

solution.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_use_locators(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://demo.playwright.dev/todomvc')
    
    input_by_css = page.locator('.new-todo')
    expect(input_by_css).to_be_visible()
    
    input_by_placeholder = page.get_by_placeholder('What needs to be done?')
    input_by_placeholder.fill('First Todo')
    input_by_placeholder.press('Enter')
    
    todo_by_text = page.get_by_text('First Todo')
    expect(todo_by_text).to_be_visible()
    
    input_by_placeholder.fill('Second Todo')
    input_by_placeholder.press('Enter')
    
    todo_list = page.locator('.todo-list')
    first_todo = todo_list.locator('li').first
    expect(first_todo).to_contain_text('First Todo')
    
    filtered_todo = page.locator('.todo-list li').filter(has_text='Second')
    expect(filtered_todo).to_be_visible()

hints: page.getByText() finds elements by visible text
Chain locators with .locator() for nested elements
Use .first(), .last(), .nth() to select from multiple matches

testCases.name: Chained locators work
testCases.expectedBehavior: Nested element found using chained locators
